learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate the consistent application of the AASW Code of Ethics (2020) to all aspects of practice in the placement agency, identifying and responding to ethical dilemmas as they arise.
Contribute to the placement organisation’s respectful incorporation of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander cultural and experiential knowledge in both practice and service design.
Demonstrate the ability to develop in collaboration with clients, and undertake, interventions informed by anti-oppressive principles addressing both individual and structural concerns.
Articulate critical reflections on the impact of personal perspectives and organisational policies into the needs of clients with diverse cultural and personal identities.
Articulate a developed, reflective practice framework incorporating an evidence-informed, client focused and critical perspective applicable across multiple practice fields.
Critically review professional decisions to identify challenges between client-led outcomes and professional, holistic assessments.
Maintain professionalism, demonstrating evidence-informed knowledge of the field while actively engaging with and seeking out learning and development opportunities.

Proactively arrange, prepare for and prioritise regular professional supervision in which themes of critical reflection onpractice, the use of self and positional power are topics of discussion in the context of a balanced agenda of education, support and accountability.

assessments.task_description:
Practice Reflection 1 (500 words).

See the Social Work Practice Education Guide 2026 (sec. 4.3) for detailed information regarding this task.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Contact your UQ Liaison person if you experience any problems completing on time.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
At the approximate mid-point of the placement, the supervisor is asked to complete, and submit to the University, a mid-placement review. This will fall around week 8 of your placement. Note that this is an approximate date only: individual placement circumstances will vary. Dates and times should be negotiated with your Supervisor. This is the student's responsibility.

This provides a basis for identifying the extent of your progress and any specific learning tasks that need to be completed in the remaining portion of the placement to assist you to pass the placement.  

The review evaluates your performance against the Learning Curriculum and the course Learning Objectives.  

A template to be used in the review is available in Learning Resources on the course Blackboard site.

A PDF of the evaluation report, signed and dated by the Supervisor and co-signed/dated by the Student, must be uploaded to Turnitin by the student.

See the Social Work Practice Education Guide 2026 (sec. 4.5.1) for detailed information regarding placement evaluations.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assessment requires students to submit an electronic version of their assignment via Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Urgently contact your UQ Liaison person if you experience any problems completing on time.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Practice Reflection 2 (500 words).

See the Social Work Practice Education Guide 2026 (sec. 4.3) for detailed information regarding this task.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Contact your UQ Liaison person if you experience any problems completing on time.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date: Must be completed prior to end of Placement.
assessments.task_description:
At a point no later than the final week of the placement, the Supervisor is asked to complete a Final Placement Evaluation, using the template available in Learning Resources on the course Blackboard site. This will fall around week 18 of your placement.

Note that this is an approximate date only: individual placement circumstances will vary. Dates and times should be negotiated with your Supervisor. This is your responsibility.

The review evaluates your performance against the Learning Curriculum and the course Learning Objectives.  

The review should be a discussion between Student and Supervisor, in which the student's performance and development across the entirety of the placement are reflected upon and evaluated as either Pass or Fail.

A PDF of the evaluation report, signed and dated by the Supervisor and the Student, will be uploaded to Turnitin by the student.

See the Social Work Practice Education Guide 2026 (sec. 4.5.1) for detailed information regarding placement evaluations.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assessment requires students to submit an electronic version of their assignment via Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Urgently contact your UQ Liaison person if you experience any problems completing on time.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Integration Workshop 1

Designed to facilitate your ability to link academic learning with practice.

At the core of each session will be your experiences on placement.

The workshops are a place to critically reflect and to support each other through the rewards and challenges of the field. This workshop focuses on practical arrangements for placement and your responsibilities.

To be conducted over Zoom using this link.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
